[15:00]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Farming vs Follow-ups"   NÚMEROS:  Ciclo de vendas < 30 dias (info products)   VISUAL:  Comparação visual lado a lado, follow-ups (dentro do ciclo de vendas, <30 dias) vs farming (fora do ciclo de vendas, meses), setas temporais, diferenciação clara   [15:02] So when I say follow-up, I typically refer to deals that should close within the average sales cycle. So if you're selling info, your sales cycle should be less than 30 days. So a follow-up should be on a sale that you've the deal you've token a prospect you've spoken to that should close within 30 days, right? And farming would go typically passed outside the sales cycle. So you can think of these these as leads that have been in your CRM, been in your ecosystem for months, right? [17:22]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Low-Hanging Fruit Concept"   NÚMEROS:  Milhares de leads já no CRM   VISUAL:  Conceito visual de fruta baixa vs alta, leads existentes no CRM destacados como oportunidade imediata vs perseguir novas chamadas agendadas, gráfico ou ilustração de oportunidade   [17:24] This is the concept of lowhanging fruit. Friends, you have a thousand leads. Your clients have a thousands of leads, tens of thousands. People always think that lowhanging fruit in sales is the book call, but that's further from the truth. That's like the one of the furthest things from the truth, right? When someone books a call, yeah, I mean, they're qualified, yes, you know, but it doesn't mean that they're financially qualified. Doesn't mean that they actually want your solution. And also, they might not even show up to the call in the first place, right? And they're usually booking one, two, three days out. So, are they really the most qualified leads?   [18:04] Lowhanging fruit refers to all of that money you spent on ad spend to generate thousands of leads, right? And you already have them in your CRM, but no one is speaking to them.   [18:15]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  Analogia do Tanque de Gasolina (referência a Jeremy)   NÚMEROS:  50 galões total, 45 galões vazando   VISUAL:  Ilustração de tanque de gasolina com vazamento, representação visual de desperdício de recursos (leads/dinheiro), comparação com funil de vendas vazando   [18:17] It's like what Jeremy said earlier today. He said, "If you got a a car or a truck that has like a 50 gallon gas tank, right, and you put in 50 gallons of gas, but it leaks 45 gallons, is that really a working car, working vehicle, right?" No, it's not. Same thing with your sales organization today. You've got these sales teams, you've got all these leads, you're spending money on ad spend, but it's like money's coming in. Leads are coming in, but then everything's just getting wasted. So, you're running a business like this, and no wonder you don't scale, right? No wonder you don't close more deals. So, today, we're going to plug these licky buckets.   [18:55] Who would agree that more um book calls would equal more sales? Anyone agrees to that? [20:05]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Situações Mudam - NO hoje ≠ NO para sempre"   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados   VISUAL:  Conceito temporal, setas mostrando mudança de "No" para "Yes" ao longo do tempo, linha do tempo de vida do prospect, mudanças de circunstâncias   [20:07] And then this is like a a big a big shift that we all have to get. As your prospects days go by, as their life changes, right, as they start, you know, they might be a no today, does that mean they're a no forever? Right. Exactly, Nick. They would eventually become a yes. So with all those leads in your CRM, if you're just leaving there, leaving them there and not doing anything with them, you're basically saying, "Hey, you know what? You're not going to buy from us today. Hey, you're not going to buy from us for forever, so I'm just going to ignore you and just forget about you, right? That and that's that's not smart. That's not the way to do it. People's situation changes all the time. It's going to go from no to yes.   [20:44]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Email Marketing vs Human to Human (H2H)"   NÚMEROS:  Grant Cardone: 3 emails/dia mencionado   VISUAL:  Comparação visual entre emails de marketing automatizados vs outreach personalizado de vendedores, contraste entre percepção de automação vs contato humano real   [20:46] Okay, but you'll be like, "But Joel, I got this thing called email marketing though, right? Like we send a daily email. It's from my face, Brandon Carter. I send a daily email to my list. That's pretty good, right?" You know, Grant Cardone sends daily emails to me. I get his email three times a day. That's pretty good. Look, let's be real. You cannot beat H to H, human to human. And that's the power of farming. Human to human. I'm not saying that Brandon isn't a human, but prospects are sophisticated. They know marketing emails are marketing emails. But it's different when they receive something from your sales professionals, right? When they perceive it to be more real.   [21:24] So what happens is that typically makes your email marketers cry because they're like well you know we can't we can't beat these sales professionals. So we always have a combination of both marketing emails and sales outreach manual or not manual but automated sales outreach that looks personalized.   [21:41]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  Case Study - Brandon Carter's Team   NÚMEROS:  Não especificados neste momento   VISUAL:  GIF animado engraçado de Brandon Carter, transição para caso real de cliente, setup para demonstração prática   [21:43] Now client case study he's going to love this. [36:49]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Filosofia de Liderança Militar"   NÚMEROS:  Não aplicável   VISUAL:  Quote de lieutenant colonel: Se equipe falha = culpa do líder, Se equipe vence = crédito para a equipe, princípio de liderança aplicado a vendas   [36:50] Yes. Credit credit goes to the team. Yeah. It's not you. It's not me actually. It's actually credit to the team. I learned that in the military from one of my lieutenant colonels. The quote that basically says, um, if your team makes a mistake, if your team fails, then it's all on you. But when your team wins and when you get great results, give the credit all back to your team. And that holds very very true and that's how we should be leading our sales teams as well. Have you given up on losing weight yet? Have you given up on building a profitable online fitness business for Brandon Carter? Here's how Kelly or here's how Dea bought her new Mercedes. Right? These are new subject lines that you can just put in your emails. [40:04]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Como o Bot Funciona"   NÚMEROS:  Exemplo: 3:45 PM   VISUAL:  Bot encontra horários abertos nos calendários da equipe durante farming, mensagens automáticas mas personalizadas, prospects podem escolher horários, agiliza trabalho dos setters   [40:06] And this would speed up so much more time because it's going to find open spots in your team's calendars while you're farming. So imagine you combine this with farming. Now you have a bot that's helping your setters find more time to get book calls for themselves using a bot that's going to message everybody personally now. So when you combine all this together, there should be no excuse why we don't even have time for farming. Okay.   [40:32]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Bot Interface Example"   NÚMEROS:  8 (opção de horário), 3:45 PM   VISUAL:  Screenshot do bot em ação: envia lista de horários disponíveis baseado em calendários da equipe, prospect escolhe "8 for 3:45 PM", automaticamente agendado   [40:34] Um this is what the bot looks like. [41:00]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Segmentation: Sniper vs Shotgun"   NÚMEROS:  6,000 pessoas (não vai ligar para todas randomicamente)   VISUAL:  Comparação visual: abordagem sniper (precisa, segmentada) vs shotgun (aleatória, spray and pray), Brandon's team como exemplo   [41:02] Segmentation. sniper versus shotgun approach when you're just calling everybody and anybody like look Brandon's team we we're not going to call 6,000 people right randomly we're not so we're just going to segment the list and you can take a photo of this too this is the way we do it   [41:20]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Hierarchy de Segmentação"   NÚMEROS:  29 email clicks, 1,300 email opens mencionados no exemplo   VISUAL:  Pirâmide ou lista hierárquica de prioridades: 1) Book calls (maior prioridade), 2) Replies positivas, 3) Email clicks, 4) Email opens - trabalhar nesta ordem, não ligar randomicamente   [41:22] obviously the people that book a call are the highest priority you're going to talk to them anyways right people that book a call people that reply positively like yeah I want to I'm interested let tell me more information you can go to people click on emails and people open emails too.   [41:37]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "GoHighLevel Analytics Example"   NÚMEROS:  29 email clicks, 1,300 email opens   VISUAL:  Screenshot do GoHighLevel mostrando métricas reais: 29 clicks, 1,300 opens, demonstração de como priorizar quem ligar baseado em engajamento   [41:39] For example, right here, go high level, right? 29 email clicks, 1,300 email opens. So, 1300 email opens, 29 email clicks. As a sales professional, I'm going to call these guys first. I'm not just going to call random people.   [41:55]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "O Problema: Sales Leaders Fazendo Equipe Ligar Random"   NÚMEROS:  Não aplicável   VISUAL:  Crítica direta: maior problema é líderes fazendo equipe ligar para pessoas aleatórias, solução inteligente é ligar apenas para quem abriu/clicou emails   [41:57] The biggest issue is sales leaders are making sales people call random people. Just call the people that have open and click emails. That's the smart way to do it, right? Okay.   [42:07]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Case Final: Brandon Carter - Matemática Real"   NÚMEROS: Início: 6,700 leads 20% dos 29 clicks = ~6 book calls 3% dos 1,300 opens = ~39 book calls Total: 47 book calls Close rate: 25% (conservador, Brian normalmente 50%) Resultado: 10 novos deals Valor por deal: $5,000-10,000 TOTAL: $50,000-100,000 em revenue esta semana   VISUAL:  Breakdown matemático completo em tela, calculadora visual mostrando conversão desde leads até revenue, apenas do PRIMEIRO email (lembre: sequência tem 30-90 dias)   [42:09] And let's do let's wrap it up with this. We started with 6,700 leads. Brandon 6.7K. We've tested that, right? We're going to get 20% of the 29 people click an email to book a call. I'm going to fix book calls. We're going to get 3% of the 1300 people that open the email to book a call. 41 book calls. Keep in mind this is just from your first email, one email. We've got 10. We're going to build it out to 30 to 90. Okay? This is driven from the first one. We're going to get 47 book calls. Brian, he typically closes at 50%. But let's say with no show, he was only going to close 25% of 47 calls. Brandon would have 10 new high ticket trainer deals that are worth between $50,000 and $100 revenue cash play this week. This coming week.   [42:58]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  "Promessa Cumprida: 5-6 Figures Esta Semana"   NÚMEROS:  $50,000-100,000 esta semana   VISUAL:  Reiteração da promessa inicial cumprida, destaque em negrito para os números, validação da estratégia com case real   [43:00] So friends, this is how you can add five to six figures in cash in your business this week. Okay, cool.   [43:07]  📊 SLIDE DETECTADO ─────────────────────────────  TEXTO:  QR Code Final   NÚMEROS:  Não aplicável   VISUAL:  QR code grande e escaneável na tela para audiência capturar, acesso a: templates, recording da apresentação, scripts, slides completos   [43:09] And thank you. We're going to end up with your uh just if you want to scan this code, you can get all the templates, the recording, the scripts, and also going to send you the slides as well. Okay. Thank you.     📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO   NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS: $40 milhões em coaching/info sales em um ano (Dan Lok) 110 profissionais de vendas na equipe 10,000+ estudantes no High Ticket Closer Certification 1,000+ training calls realizados $15,000 cobrado por implementação desta estratégia 4-5x ROI em uma semana $1 milhão/mês alcançado em 4 meses (Two Comma Club) Taylor: $18K (outubro) → $250K (novembro) vendendo para Tony Robbins Brandon Carter: 100,000 leads, $1.5-2 milhões gastos em ad spend Case Study Real: 6,700 leads → 47 book calls → 10 deals → $50,000-100,000 em uma semana 100 replies em 30 minutos do primeiro email 29 email clicks, 1,300 email opens Close rate conservador: 25% (Brian normalmente 50%) GoHighLevel: $99/mês Sequências: 30-90 dias com 1 dia de intervalo   ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:   FARMING vs FOLLOW-UPS: Follow-ups: deals dentro do ciclo de vendas (<30 dias) Farming: leads fora do ciclo de vendas (meses no CRM) Foco em reativar leads "mortos" que já foram pagos via ad spend   4 PASSOS PARA CONSTRUIR SUA FARM: Passo 1:  Vender a ideia para a equipe (farming > esperar book calls) Passo 2:  Tornar fácil de fazer (sem adicionar fricção) Passo 3:  Trabalhar com email & SMS copy (usar ChatGPT) Passo 4:  SEGMENTAÇÃO (não ligar para todos randomicamente)   PRO TIPS DE IMPLEMENTAÇÃO: Adicionar no-shows e no-closes às sequências Emails DEVEM vir de sales rep desconhecido, NÃO da marca/CEO Incluir SMS além de emails Construir sequências de 30-90 dias automatizadas Usar GoHighLevel ou HubSpot para automação   HIERARCHY DE SEGMENTAÇÃO (Sniper vs Shotgun): Prioridade 1: Book calls agendadas Prioridade 2: Replies positivas Prioridade 3: Email clicks (29 no caso) Prioridade 4: Email opens (1,300 no caso) Não ligar randomicamente - trabalhar por ordem de engajamento   TECNOLOGIA ESSENCIAL: CRM obrigatório (GoHighLevel $99/mês ou HubSpot) Capturar números de telefone nos opt-ins AI Appointment Booking Bot (crédito: Zach) Automação de emails e SMS Capacidade de enviar imagens/screenshots via SMS   CONTENT STRATEGY: Subject lines testados: "Have you given up on...", "Here's how [nome] bought Mercedes", "Why not making 10K/month sucks" Email + SMS combinados Incluir case studies com screenshots Pode adicionar vídeos Conteúdo + CTAs diários   ESTRUTURA:   FILOSOFIA DE LIDERANÇA: Se negócio falha = culpa do líder Se equipe não fecha = culpa do líder Se equipe não quer fazer farming = líder não vendeu a visão Crédito de sucesso = sempre para o team "You can either be right or you can be rich" (Jeremy Haynes)   PROBLEMA IDENTIFICADO: 50%+ do tempo da equipe gasto em Non-Sales Activities (NSAs) Fat cat closers esperando por inbound calls Leads "vazando" do funil (analogia do tanque de gasolina) $2 milhões em ad spend com oportunidades não maximizadas Prospects desaparecem em "buraco negro" após no-show   MUDANÇA DE MENTALIDADE: Low-hanging fruit = leads já no CRM, NÃO book calls novas Sales é relacional, não transacional (conceito Rolodex) NO hoje ≠ NO para sempre (situações mudam) H2H (Human to Human) > Email marketing automatizado Tecnologia = arma essencial (lanças vs rifles)   ANATOMIA DO SUCESSO: Mark Foron: fazendo $40K/mês enquanto outros faziam $8K Ele "farmava" os leads de todos os 11 outros closers Tinha acesso ao 1 milhão de leads no CRM da Dan Lok Sequenciava todos os prospects sistematicamente   DADOS FINANCEIROS: Investimento:  $0 para reativar leads já pagos Custo de implementação:  $99/mês (GoHighLevel) ou similar ROI:  4-5x em uma semana Resultado garantido:  5-6 figuras esta semana Case Brandon Carter:  $50K-100K de uma única sequência no primeiro email Potencial:  30-90 dias de sequência = múltiplos ciclos de revenue Lifetime value:  Relacionamentos de longo prazo como Rolodex   RECURSOS DISPONIBILIZADOS: QR code com templates de email e SMS Recording da apresentação Scripts plug and play Slides completos Video separado com matemática detalhada para sales professionals     DURAÇÃO:  ~43 minutos FORMATO:  Apresentação ao vivo em mastermind, case studies reais, demonstração de resultados com cliente presente (Brandon Carter)
